column1: # mapping_set_title: Open Policy Agent (OPA) to SLSA Mappings
# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between Open Policy Agent (OPA)
#   LinkML classes and the SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts)
#   LinkML schema. OPA bundles are signed, versioned policy artefacts whose
#   delivery and verification semantics map directly onto SLSA's provenance
#   skos:closeMatch where the two classes describe the same provenance
#   artefact (e.g. WasmResolver as a ResourceDescriptor for a wasm-compiled
#   policy entrypoint, or the signed Manifest envelope as an in-toto
#   Statement); skos:relatedMatch where the OPA class carries metadata of a
#   SLSA concern (e.g. Manifest digests for VSA verification, Policy as the
#   built Package); skos:broadMatch where the OPA class is a container that

column10: A signed bundle Manifest is the equivalent of an in-toto Statement envelope binding subject(s) to a predicate (here, the bundle contents and roots)
A Manifest carries the equivalent of build provenance about a bundle - revision, signing, roots - aligning with the SLSA BuildProvenance predicate type
Manifest signing data and verification metadata parallel a SLSA verification summary attestation
A Manifest carries (or is signed against) a digest set covering the bundle's payload files
A WasmResolver binds a logical policy path to a concrete wasm artefact - the same role as a SLSA ResourceDescriptor identifying a named resource
A Manifest is a container that describes many resources (roots, wasm modules); broader than any single ResourceDescriptor
A compiled IR Policy is the built artefact produced from Rego source code, parallel to a SLSA Package as the unit of build output
